mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power with a t-statistic below 1.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline when either series declines, or 60-day forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive equity returns over 20 trading days specifically during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 52% or Rank IC turning negative.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust economic activity and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistical correlation with 5-day forward returns with a p-value above 0.10, or positive return frequency falls below 50% across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index readings above 258 indicate persistent inflationary pressure that forces monetary tightening expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of high-inflation observations, or the inflation threshold loses predictive significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and consumption signal yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during late-cycle expansions signal manufacturing contraction and inventory drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, with positive return frequency exceeding 60% or Rank IC remaining below 0.01.
